reviewedFeatures.summary: The patch seeds `currentMessageId` for the helper-level happy path, but the real selection flow still does one SessionStore read for seeding and a second read for `ChatPanel.switchToContext()`, so async bridge persistence can leave the hydrated panel snapshot ahead of the live relay and the next `text_delta` can still fork a new row.

blockingIssues.description: `selectScoop()` seeds `OffscreenClient.currentMessageId` via `reconcileForScoopSelection()` before `ChatPanel.switchToContext()` loads the chat session, but those are two independent SessionStore reads. Because `OffscreenBridge.persistScoop()` writes are fire-and-forget, the two reads can observe different snapshots: the panel can hydrate an in-progress assistant message that `currentMessageId` never seeds, so the next `text_delta` still forks a fresh row.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Drive selection-time seeding from the same session snapshot that `ChatPanel.switchToContext()` renders (or thread the hydrated in-progress message id through the selection flow) so `currentMessageId` cannot lag behind the panel after async `persistScoop()` writes.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to record that selection-time `currentMessageId` seeding must use the same SessionStore snapshot that `ChatPanel.switchToContext()` renders; separate pre-hydration loads can race with async `persistScoop()` writes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the precedence between the generic manual CDP verification requirement and feature-level instructions that mark browser checks optional. If a worker skips the generic check because a feature narrows it, the handoff should mark `followedProcedure: false` unless the skill explicitly codifies that exception.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: This review found `features.json` marked manual CDP verification OPTIONAL for `misc-reconcile-offscreen-client-currentmessageid-on-selection`, the worker skipped browser checks, and the handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true`. A similar mismatch was already captured in core-followup scrutiny for `fix-agent-scoop-visibility`, so the ambiguity is recurring rather than one-off.
